works.title: On the Origin of Species by Means of Natural Selection
works.why_included: Grounds P1 — Darwin establishes the survival-and-reproduction loss function that P1 formalizes. Primary extractions carry Darwin's verbatim statements from Chapters III (Struggle for Existence) and IV (Natural Selection + Sexual Selection).

works.title: The Study of Instinct
works.why_included: Grounds DA4 — Tinbergen's founding articulation of sign stimuli, innate releasing mechanisms, and supernormal stimuli. Extractions carry Tinbergen's verbatim statements from Chapter III of the proxy-hijacking logic that DA4 formalizes.

works.title: The Genetical Evolution of Social Behaviour (I and II)
works.why_included: Grounds P1 — Hamilton's formal derivation of inclusive fitness and Hamilton's rule, extending Darwin's loss function to kin-selected altruism. Directly forces P1.

works.title: Attachment and Loss, Volume 1: Attachment
works.why_included: Grounds DA7 and M3 — Bowlby's founding articulation of attachment as a primary evolved motivational system and the environment of evolutionary adaptedness (EEA) concept itself, which P3 and DA7 both rest on.

works.title: Paleolithic Nutrition: A Consideration of Its Nature and Current Implications
works.why_included: Grounds P3 — Eaton & Konner's discordance hypothesis in NEJM is the founding statement of the ancestral-vs-modern environment gap and the template all subsequent mismatch analyses follow.

works.title: The Adapted Mind: Evolutionary Psychology and the Generation of Culture
works.authors: Jerome H. Barkow, Leda Cosmides, John Tooby (editors)
works.why_included: Grounds P2 and DA6 — Tooby & Cosmides' founding statement of evolutionary psychology's design-principle framework: evolved mechanisms are content-specific, domain-specialized adaptations to Pleistocene adaptive problems, with explicit EEA-vs-modern-environment discordance.

works.title: Stress and the Individual: Mechanisms Leading to Disease
works.why_included: Grounds DC1 — McEwen & Stellar's original allostasis/allostatic-load formulation: the organism maintains stability through dynamic adjustment, and the cumulative cost of that adjustment under chronic challenge is the pathway to disease.

works.title: Affective Neuroscience: The Foundations of Human and Animal Emotions
works.why_included: Grounds DA8 — the original Panksepp textbook establishing the subcortical affective systems methodology that Archaeology of Mind later popularized. Precedes and grounds M1-M4 and M14.

works.title: Protective and damaging effects of stress mediators
works.why_included: Grounds DC1 and DA7 — McEwen's NEJM review: stress mediators are protective short-term via allostasis but produce cumulative allostatic load when chronically elevated. The founding clinical statement of allostatic load.

works.title: Relationship of Childhood Abuse and Household Dysfunction to Many of the Leading Causes of Death in Adults
works.authors: Felitti VJ, Anda RF, Nordenberg D, et al.
works.why_included: Grounds DA3 — Felitti et al. ACE Study establishes the dose-response relationship between adverse childhood experience categories and adult health outcomes across fundamentally different domains, empirical anchor for recurrent coupling and cascading.

works.title: The Role of Dopamine in Incentive Salience
works.why_included: Grounds DA4 — Berridge & Robinson's founding statement of the wanting/liking dissociation and incentive salience. The mechanism by which proxies can hijack motivational systems independent of hedonic satisfaction.

works.title: Hierarchy in the Forest: The Evolution of Egalitarian Behavior
works.why_included: Grounds M11 and DA6 — Boehm's reverse-dominance hypothesis: egalitarian hunter-gatherer bands maintain equality through active coalitional suppression of upstarts, demonstrating that humans carry both dominance and anti-dominance motives as partially competing programs.

works.title: Mother Nature: A History of Mothers, Infants, and Natural Selection
works.why_included: Load-bears M9 Care/Alloparenting (primary target, currently 10 rows after Bullmore bump). Mother Nature is Hrdy s comprehensive synthesis of cooperative breeding, maternal ambivalence, life-history variation in maternal investment, and the evolutionary ecology of mother-infant relationships across primates and humans. It preceded her 2009 Mothers and Others by a decade and covers substantially different material. Also supports M14 Reproductive Motivation (maternal/sexual overlap), P1 Inclusive Fitness (kin-selection maternal-investment tradeoffs), and DA7 Developmental Calibration (infant-rearing variation shaping adult architecture).

works.title: Stability, Precision, and Near-24-Hour Period of the Human Circadian Pacemaker
works.authors: Charles A. Czeisler, Jeanne F. Duffy, Theresa L. Shanahan, Emery N. Brown, Jude F. Mitchell, David W. Rimmer, Joseph M. Ronda, Edward J. Silva, James S. Allan, Jonathan S. Emens, Derk-Jan Dijk, Richard E. Kronauer
works.why_included: Grounds M7 — Czeisler et al.'s forced-desynchrony protocol establishing human intrinsic circadian period at ~24.18 hours, not the previously-believed 25 hours. Foundational measurement for M7.

works.title: Error Management Theory: A New Perspective on Biases in Cross-Sex Mind Reading
works.why_included: Grounds DA5 — Haselton & Buss's founding statement of Error Management Theory: asymmetric error costs produce predictable domain-specific biases.

works.title: Is Depression an Adaptation?
works.why_included: Grounds DA1 — Nesse's foundational case for depression subtypes as evolved defensive signals (social defeat, pursuit of unreachable goals, kin loss, incapacitation when physically ill). Predates Good Reasons for Bad Feelings (2019) by 19 years and is THE primary journal-article statement of the defensive-signal thesis applied to depression. Published in Archives of General Psychiatry 57:14-20.

works.title: Altruistic Punishment in Humans
works.why_included: Grounds M11 — Fehr & Gächter's experimental demonstration of altruistic punishment: humans pay personal costs to punish free-riders with no kin, reciprocity, or reputation benefits. Empirical anchor for the norm-enforcement component of cooperation.

works.title: Disgust as an Adaptive System for Disease Avoidance Behaviour
works.authors: Curtis V, Aunger R, Rabie T
works.why_included: Grounds M12 / DA5 — Curtis et al.'s evolved-disgust-as-disease-avoidance framework. Primary source for treating disgust as a behavioral immune system with smoke-detector calibration and pregnancy-modulated sensitivity.

works.title: Epigenetic programming by maternal behavior
works.authors: Weaver ICG, Cervoni N, Champagne FA, D'Alessio AC, Sharma S, Seckl JR, Dymov S, Szyf M, Meaney MJ
works.why_included: Grounds DA7 and M3 — Weaver/Meaney's demonstration that maternal care programs glucocorticoid-receptor gene expression via epigenetic methylation, and that cross-fostering reverses the programming — direct evidence for developmental calibration with environmental reversibility.

works.title: Not by Genes Alone: How Culture Transformed Human Evolution
works.authors: Peter J. Richerson and Robert Boyd
works.why_included: Grounds P3 constraint — Richerson & Boyd's formal statement of gene-culture coevolution: culture is itself an adaptation that shapes the expected environment, so 'matched' must include cultural scaffolding, not just ancestral surface form.

works.title: Mothers and Others: The Evolutionary Origins of Mutual Understanding
works.why_included: Grounds M9 and DA2 — Hrdy's cooperative-breeding thesis: human infants evolved under an alloparenting regime where survival depended on eliciting care from multiple caregivers, making social-scaffolding-of-regulation constitutive rather than optional.

works.title: How Many Friends Does One Person Need? Dunbar's Number and Other Evolutionary Quirks
works.why_included: Grounds DA2 — Dunbar's consolidated statement of the social-brain hypothesis and the ~150-person cognitive ceiling on coherent face-to-face relationships, providing the parameter value for the social architecture DA2 rests on.

works.title: Addiction by Design
works.why_included: Grounds DC2 and DA4 — Schüll's ethnography of engineered addiction in machine gambling. Primary source for the 'machine zone' dissociation and the industry's structural dependence on problem gamblers (30-60% of revenue).

works.title: Stress and anxiety: Structural plasticity and epigenetic regulation as a consequence of stress
works.authors: McEwen, Bruce S.; Eiland, Lisa; Hunter, Richard G.; Miller, Melinda M.
works.why_included: Grounds DC1, DA1, and DA7 — McEwen et al.'s 2012 review consolidates the structural-plasticity evidence for reversible remodeling of hippocampus, prefrontal cortex, and amygdala under chronic stress. Critical for Cor because it supports the 'much wear reverses with environmental correction' claim in DC1's scope notes and the 'defensive activation vs damage' distinction in DA1: stress-induced structural remodeling is adaptive plasticity, not brain damage, except under sustained overload.

works.title: The Archaeology of Mind: Neuroevolutionary Origins of Human Emotions
works.why_included: Grounds DA8 — Panksepp's mature statement of seven conserved subcortical affective-motivational systems (SEEKING, FEAR, RAGE, LUST, CARE, PANIC/GRIEF, PLAY) with cross-species phylogenetic evidence for their priority over cortical processes. Sources for M1, M2, M3, M4, M14.

works.title: The Story of the Human Body: Evolution, Health, and Disease
works.why_included: Lieberman's flagship statement of the evolutionary-mismatch framework preceding Exercised (2021). This is the foundational book-length articulation of mismatch as a diagnostic lens for modern chronic disease, covering the agricultural transition, the industrial transition, and the specific mismatch etiology of obesity, type 2 diabetes, cardiovascular disease, and musculoskeletal disease. Primary pillar for Lieberman's contribution to P3 Systematic Mismatch and M13 Energy Regulation at the book-length evidentiary level. Where Exercised focuses on movement specifically, Story of the Human Body establishes the broader mismatch framework that M13 applies to food, metabolism, and the farming-transition energy cascade.

works.title: A Natural History of Human Thinking
works.why_included: Grounds M11 — Tomasello's shared-intentionality hypothesis: human cognition evolved via two transitions into joint and collective intentionality structures that made cooperative problem-solving and cultural institutions possible.

works.title: Touch
works.why_included: Tiffany Field's canonical synthesis of the Touch Research Institute program. This is the primary-source book for R1 Touch in the mechanism architecture — Field's summary of touch-deprivation developmental consequences, massage therapy empirical effects (especially premature-infant weight gain and cortisol reduction), touch in adult attachment and stress regulation, and cultural variation in touch frequency. Tagged pillar because Field is the single most influential researcher on touch as a regulatory input and this book is her accessible synthesis of decades of Touch Research Institute work plus the broader comparative literature (Harlow, Jourard, Montagu, Romanian orphanage studies).

works.title: Interface Theory of Perception
works.authors: Hoffman DD, Singh M, Prakash C
works.why_included: Grounds OF1 — Hoffman, Singh & Prakash's Interface Theory of Perception formalizes the claim that perception is a species-specific fitness interface, not a veridical window on objective reality.

works.title: Social Baseline Theory: The Social Regulation of Human Body and Brain
works.why_included: Grounds DA2 — Coan & Sbarra's Social Baseline Theory: the brain's default expectation is proximity to trusted others, and isolation is the energetically costly deviation rather than the reverse. Extractions carry Coan & Sbarra's verbatim formulation of the social-regulation hypothesis that DA2 rests on.

works.title: Constrained Total Energy Expenditure and Metabolic Adaptation to Physical Activity in Adult Humans
works.authors: Pontzer, Herman; Durazo-Arvizu, Ramon; Dugas, Lara R.; Plange-Rhule, Jacob; Bovet, Pascal; Forrester, Terrence E.; Lambert, Estelle V.; Cooper, Richard S.; Schoeller, Dale A.; Luke, Amy
works.why_included: Grounds M13 Energy Regulation at the primary-source level. This is the foundational multi-population (n=332, five countries) doubly-labeled-water study establishing the constrained total energy expenditure model: TEE plateaus above moderate activity levels rather than rising linearly. The paper is load-bearing for M13's cascade framing and for the evidence that human metabolism is homeostatically buffered against habitual activity variation — exercise alone does not produce linear caloric deficits, because the body adapts to maintain TEE within a narrow range. Tagged as pillar because this is the canonical Current Biology paper that introduced the Constrained TEE model to the literature.

works.title: The Secret of Our Success: How Culture is Driving Human Evolution, Domesticating Our Species, and Making Us Smarter
works.why_included: Grounds P3 constraint and P2 — Henrich's formal case that cumulative cultural adaptation IS part of human biology, not separate from it. Cultural transmission is what domain-sensitive evolved cognition produces, not an alternative to it. Complements WEIRDest People 2020 on the specific-culture end by grounding the general cultural-evolution claim, and provides the Franklin Expedition case (the canonical P3 constraint demonstration).

works.title: Behave: The Biology of Humans at Our Best and Worst
works.why_included: Grounds M5 and DC1 — Sapolsky's synthesis of hierarchy physiology and stress-reactivity data, including the Forest Troop natural experiment (Sapolsky & Share 2004) demonstrating that hierarchy type (not its mere existence) determines pathology, and that environmental correction can shift a baboon troop's cultural style.

works.title: How Emotions Are Made: The Secret Life of the Brain
works.why_included: Grounds Bridge Thesis (Panksepp-Barrett resolution) — Barrett's theory of constructed emotion provides the critique of folk emotion categories as natural kinds. In Cor she does not displace Panksepp's subcortical systems but scopes them: the primary-process affective systems are real; the named categories (sadness, fear, anger) are culturally constructed overlays.

works.title: The Mismatch Hypothesis: What Can We Learn from Hunter-Gatherers?
works.authors: Li NP, van Vugt M, Colarelli SM
works.why_included: Grounds P3 — Li, van Vugt & Colarelli provide the four-step mismatch identification framework and the forced-vs-hijacked mismatch distinction.

works.title: Good Reasons for Bad Feelings: Insights from the Frontier of Evolutionary Psychiatry
works.why_included: Grounds DA1 — Nesse's systematic treatment of negative emotions as evolved defensive signals rather than disorders, including the Smoke Detector Principle quantitative derivation that also anchors DA5.

works.title: The WEIRDest People in the World: How the West Became Psychologically Peculiar and Particularly Prosperous
works.why_included: Grounds P3 — Henrich's documentation that Western populations anchor the extreme end of the cross-cultural distribution on major psychological measures, providing the empirical diagnostic frame for evaluating mismatch: modern WEIRD environments are not the typical human range.

works.title: Fitness Beats Truth in the Evolution of Perception
works.authors: Prakash C, Stephens KD, Hoffman DD, Singh M, Fields C
works.why_included: Grounds OF1 — Prakash et al. Fitness-Beats-Truth theorem proves that selection drives veridical perception strategies to extinction as world complexity grows.

works.title: The Status Syndrome: How Social Standing Affects Our Health and Longevity
works.why_included: Grounds M5 Status Monitoring (primary) with secondary links to M6 Controllability/Agency and DA6 Competing Motivational Programs. Marmot's 2004 synthesis of the Whitehall I (1976 onward) and Whitehall II (1985 onward) longitudinal civil-servant cohort studies establishes the 'status syndrome': a gradient of mortality and morbidity that follows occupational grade even within white-collar populations, not reducible to poverty, with autonomy/control and social participation as the operationally identified causal mechanism. Work is tiered 'key' because the book is a synthesis for general audiences; the primary data live in the underlying Whitehall papers, which remain a source gap pending direct extraction. Cor uses this work as the canonical statement of the hierarchy-physiology-to-health pathway.

works.title: The Debate over Dopamine's Role in Reward: The Case for Incentive Salience
works.why_included: Grounds DA4 — Berridge's 2007 restatement of the wanting/liking dissociation with updated human neuroimaging evidence from Parkinson's DDS patients and healthy-volunteer dietary and pharmacological dopamine manipulations. The 1998 Berridge & Robinson original remains a source gap pending direct extraction; the 2007 paper is the current anchor for DA4's incentive-salience claim.

works.title: Loneliness: Human Nature and the Need for Social Connection
works.why_included: Grounds M3 Social Bonding (primary) with secondary links to M1 Threat Management and M10 Movement/Regulatory. Cacioppo & Patrick's 2008 synthesis is the canonical statement of the loneliness-as-evolved-signal research program: perceived social isolation is an aversive signal selected to motivate re-connection, with downstream physiological consequences (hypervigilance, elevated cortisol, dampened ventral-striatum reward response, inflammatory gene-expression changes, elevated total peripheral resistance). Work is tiered 'key' not 'pillar' because it is a synthesis for lay audiences; the underlying primary data live in Cacioppo's Ohio State / Chicago lab papers and in collaborative publications with Hawkley, Cole, and Boomsma.

works.title: The Playful Brain: Venturing to the Limits of Neuroscience
works.why_included: Load-bears M4 Social Calibration/Play (primary target, currently 3 rows after Lieberman bump — critical weakness still). Pellis & Pellis are named in M4 source_researchers and The Playful Brain is their synthesis of 25+ years of comparative play research across mammalian taxa, with detailed neurobiological substrate claims (prefrontal cortex, amygdala, somatosensory cortex, opioid/dopamine circuits). This book is the single most important source for grounding M4 at the neurobiological-and-comparative levels. Also supports DA8 Phylogenetic Priority (play across mammals) and M9 Care/Alloparenting (mother-infant play bouts). Work is tiered key because Playful Brain is a 2009 synthesis for general/graduate audiences; the underlying empirical papers (Pellis lab publications) live in the peer-reviewed neuroscience literature and would be pillar-tier if added separately.

works.title: Allostatic load biomarkers of chronic stress and impact on health and cognition
works.authors: Juster, Robert-Paul; McEwen, Bruce S.; Lupien, Sonia J.
works.why_included: Grounds DC1 and DA3 — Juster, McEwen & Lupien's 2010 review operationalizes allostatic load into a measurable multi-systemic biomarker index (cortisol, catecholamines, DHEA-S, cholesterol, HbA1c, CRP, fibrinogen, blood pressure, waist-to-hip ratio) and reviews dozens of studies linking elevated AL to mortality, cognitive decline, and cardiovascular disease. Complements the 1998 NEJM and 1993 McEwen & Stellar formulations with a matured, empirically-validated version of the allostatic load framework.

works.title: Social Relationships and Mortality Risk: A Meta-analytic Review
works.authors: Holt-Lunstad J, Smith TB, Layton JB
works.why_included: Empirical landmark for DA2 — Holt-Lunstad et al. 148-study, 308,849-participant meta-analysis: strong social relationships carry a 50% mortality-reduction advantage, comparable to smoking cessation. Downstream evidence rather than a primary architectural claim, hence importance='key' not 'pillar'.

works.title: The Behavioral Immune System (and Why It Matters)
works.why_included: Grounds M12 — Schaller & Park's consolidation of the Behavioral Immune System framework: evolved psychological mechanisms detecting pathogen cues and triggering disgust-mediated avoidance on smoke-detector principles.

works.title: Hunter-Gatherer Energetics and Human Obesity
works.authors: Pontzer, Herman; Raichlen, David A.; Wood, Brian M.; Mabulla, Audax Z. P.; Racette, Susan B.; Marlowe, Frank W.
works.why_included: Grounds M13 Energy Regulation with the empirical finding that Hadza hunter-gatherer daily energy expenditure is similar to that of Western sedentary adults despite dramatically greater physical activity. The PLOS ONE paper is the original cross-population result that motivated the later 2016 Constrained TEE model (Pontzer et al. 2016). Together they establish that obesity cannot be explained simply as a physical-activity deficit in industrialized populations: Hadza and Westerners spend similar total calories per day, so the difference must lie primarily in energy intake and food composition.

works.title: Internal Time: Chronotypes, Social Jet Lag, and Why You're So Tired
works.why_included: Grounds M7 — Roenneberg's chronotype research and the social-jetlag concept: chronic discrepancy between biological and socially-imposed sleep schedules as a pervasive mismatch condition.

works.title: Oxytocin and Social Affiliation in Humans
works.why_included: Grounds R1 and M3 — Feldman's synthesis of oxytocin and human affiliation, including parent-infant bonding, romantic pair bonding, and cross-generational transmission of parenting via bio-behavioral synchrony.

works.title: Social Jetlag and Obesity
works.authors: Till Roenneberg, Karla V. Allebrandt, Martha Merrow, Celine Vetter
works.why_included: Grounds M7 — Roenneberg et al.'s demonstration of social jetlag affecting 69% of the population and predicting BMI specifically in the overweight subgroup.

works.title: Salt Sugar Fat
works.why_included: Grounds DC2 and M13 — Moss's documentation of bliss-point engineering and industry knowledge of designed satiety bypass in ultra-processed food.

works.title: Maternal-preterm skin-to-skin contact enhances child physiologic organization and cognitive control across the first 10 years of life.
works.authors: Feldman R, Rosenthal Z, Eidelman AI.
works.why_included: Empirical for R1 and ED9 — Feldman et al.'s 10-year longitudinal RCT showing kangaroo care produces durable autonomic, attachment, and cognitive benefits in preterm infants into adolescence.

works.title: Does Incidental Disgust Amplify Moral Judgment? A Meta-Analytic Review of Experimental Evidence
works.why_included: Challenge to incidental-disgust-amplifies-moral-judgment literature — Landy & Goodwin meta-analysis (k=50) finds small raw effect (d=0.11) that largely disappears after publication-bias correction. Imported to discipline M12 framing.

works.title: Evening use of light-emitting eReaders negatively affects sleep, circadian timing, and next-morning alertness
works.authors: Chang AM, Aeschbach D, Duffy JF, Czeisler CA
works.why_included: Empirical M7 — Chang et al. randomized crossover demonstrating that LE-eBook use before bed produces measurable circadian disruption, melatonin suppression, and next-morning sleepiness.

works.title: Anatomy of Love: A Natural History of Mating, Marriage, and Why We Stray
works.why_included: Grounds M14 Reproductive Motivation (primary) — the mechanism for which Fisher is the single most important researcher. Fisher's three-component architecture (lust / romantic love / attachment with distinct neurochemical substrates) is the canonical decomposition of M14 and is the explicit basis for M14's phylogenetic timing (~500Myr sexual reproduction, ~200Myr mammalian pair-bonding). Work is tiered 'key' because the book is a revised 2016 synthesis for general audiences; Fisher's primary fMRI studies (with Lucy Brown, Art Aron, Bianca Acevedo) live in the underlying peer-reviewed papers. Individual passages reporting specific fMRI results or the Swedish AVPR1A gene study are tagged source_type='primary'; framework/synthesis passages are tagged 'propagation'.

works.title: Learned Helplessness at Fifty: Insights from Neuroscience
works.why_included: Grounds M6 — Maier & Seligman's 2016 inversion: passivity is the default and control is learned via vmPFC-DRN circuit. The founding statement of M6 (Controllability/Agency) mechanism.

works.title: The Handbook of Evolutionary Psychology, Volume 1: Foundations (2nd ed.)
works.why_included: Grounds DA5 and M14 — Buss's consolidated volume covering error management, sexual strategies theory, sex-differentiated mate preferences, and the fear-module program of research. Primary source for M14 (reproductive motivation) architecture.

works.title: Why We Sleep
works.why_included: Grounds M7 — Walker's synthesis of sleep neuroscience and the consequences of circadian and sleep disruption across metabolic, immune, emotional, and memory systems.

works.title: Association between physical exercise and mental health in 1.2 million individuals in the USA between 2011 and 2015: a cross-sectional study
works.authors: Chekroud SR, Gueorguieva R, Zheutlin AB, Paulus M, Krumholz HM, Krystal JH
works.why_included: Empirical supporting M10 — Chekroud et al. 1.2M-person cross-sectional study showing exercisers have 43% fewer poor-mental-health days per month than matched non-exercisers, across all exercise types.

works.title: The Inflamed Mind: A Radical New Approach to Depression
works.why_included: Load-bears M8 Immune Regulation (primary target). Bullmore synthesizes the Dantzer / Raison / Miller / Danese neuroimmunology literature on cytokine sickness behavior, inflammation-as-cause of depression, the failed monoamine paradigm, and the stress->immune->mood cascade. The underlying primary research (Dantzer cytokine experiments, Danese ACE-inflammation longitudinal cohorts, Raison/Miller clinical trials) is tagged propagation because Bullmore is reporting rather than originating. His own first-person psychiatric clinical observations and the broader narrative framing are tagged primary. Load-bearing for M8 plus supporting contributions to M3 Social Bonding (sickness-behavior social withdrawal), M6 Controllability/Agency (sickness behavior as evolved withdrawal), and DC1 Allostatic Load (inflammation as chronic-stress mediator).

works.title: Screens, Teens, and Psychological Well-Being: Evidence From Three Time-Use-Diary Studies.
works.why_included: Challenge to strong screen-time-as-mismatch claims — Orben & Przybylski time-use diary findings showing little evidence of substantial negative associations in adolescent well-being. Imported to discipline Cor's DC2/mismatch framing: not all technology is the same, and effect sizes matter.

works.title: A meta-analysis of the association between adolescent social media use and depressive symptoms.
works.authors: Ivie EJ, Pettitt A, Moses LJ, Allen NB
works.why_included: Challenge / scope refinement — Ivie et al. meta-analysis showing small-but-significant correlation between adolescent social media use and depressive symptoms with high heterogeneity (I2=95). Cor must treat the effect as real but modest and highly moderated.

works.title: Exercised: The Science of Physical Activity, Rest and Health
works.why_included: Load-bears M10 Movement/Regulatory (primary target) — Lieberman is listed in M10 source_researchers and the M10 resolution_conditions wording (movement embedded in daily life rather than set aside as exercise) is essentially paraphrasing this book. Also supports M13 Energy Regulation (via Pontzer TEE synthesis and Hadza energetic data) and DA4 Proxy Hijacking (exercise as discretionary category error vs embedded daily activity). Work is tiered key because Exercised is a 2021 popular-science synthesis for general audiences; Lieberman's primary fieldwork papers (hunter-gatherer activity levels, endurance-running biomechanics, active-grandparent evidence) live in the underlying peer-reviewed literature. Passages reporting Lieberman's own Pemja/Hadza fieldwork or his original theoretical contributions (active grandparent hypothesis) are tagged source_type=primary; passages synthesizing Pontzer, Cooper, Fries, etc. are tagged propagation.

works.title: Darwin's "Horrid" Doubt Revisited: A Critical Reassessment of Evolutionary Arguments against Truth
works.why_included: Philosophical challenge to OF1 — Esposito's reassessment of the Evolutionary Argument Against Truth. Imported to represent the contested-philosophical-challenge axis; Cor's OF1 position survives this challenge but the argument deserves explicit treatment.

works.title: Sitting, Squatting, and the Evolutionary Biology of Human Inactivity
works.authors: Raichlen, David A.; Pontzer, Herman; Zderic, Theodore W.; Harris, Jacob A.; Mabulla, Audax Z. P.; Hamilton, Marc T.; Wood, Brian M.
works.why_included: Grounds M13 and M10 with the finding that hunter-gatherer rest postures (squatting, kneeling) maintain muscle activation in ways that modern chair-based sitting does not. Hadza adults sit for comparable hours per day to industrialized adults, but their rest postures keep leg muscles engaged in a way that generates ongoing metabolic activity. This resolves an apparent paradox — how can hunter-gatherers be the comparison case for both ACTIVE daily movement AND extensive rest — by showing that the rest posture itself differs architecturally. Load-bearing for M13's distinction between pattern-of-movement and total-energy-throughput.

works.title: Social Media Use and Internalizing Symptoms in Clinical and Community Adolescent Samples: A Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis.
works.authors: Fassi L, Thomas K, Parry DA, Leyland-Craggs A, Ford TJ, Orben A
works.why_included: Challenge / scope refinement — Fassi et al. 2024 largest-to-date meta (143 studies, N=1M+) on social media and adolescent internalizing symptoms. The effect is small but consistent; the clinical relevance depends on moderator structure, not average effect size.
